headline: US Postal Service to Hike Prices as War Drives Up Fuel Costs
summary: (Bloomberg) -- The US Postal Service plans to raise prices to offset rising transportation expenses including the cost of fuel, a sign that the beleaguered mail carrier is feeling the strain of higher oil prices as the war in Iran continues.The USPS will increase prices by 8% on some packages until mid-January of next year, the USPS said in a statement Wednesday. The change will apply to its Priority Mail, Priority Mail Express, USPS Ground Advantage and Parcel Select offerings. Other products s

headline: Shutdown Deal Loses Traction. Hope of Relief for Air Travelers, TSA Workers Is Dimming.
summary: Transportation Security Administration agents’ hopes of getting paid soon were dashed Wednesday as initial support for the latest Department of Homeland Security funding deal fizzled out.  Republican senators presented Democrats with their most promising deal yet on Tuesday to end the 38-day shutdown, which has left the security agents without pay.  The deal would fund all of DHS, which oversees TSA, except for the migrant-removal activities of Immigration and Customs Enforcement, another DHS agency.

headline: The S&P 500 Rips 1% Higher On Hope For Iran Peace Negotiations and Oil Falls Below Critical $100 Barrel Line
summary: Geopolitical hope is doing what central banks could not quite manage this month: lifting every major U.S. equity index simultaneously.
